question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (bytes field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a maximally-sized transaction payload that causes the importer to allocate an unbounded `byte[] bytes` array in a single `RecordFile` instance, exhausting JVM heap memory and crashing the importer service, halting all record file processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (items field)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the network with the maximum number of transactions per consensus round so that `List<RecordItem> items` in a single `RecordFile` grows without an enforced upper bound in the domain object, causing the importer to OOM-crash when iterating `recordFile.getItems().forEach(...)` in `RecordFileParser.doParse()`?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (sidecars field)] Can an unprivileged external user deploy a smart contract that generates the maximum number of sidecar records per block, causing `Collection<SidecarFile> sidecars` in `RecordFile` to hold an unbounded collection of large `SidecarFile` objects, exhausting heap memory in the importer and stalling all downstream parsing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (logsBloom field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call that produces a maximally-sized bloom filter output, causing `byte[] logsBloom` in `RecordFile` to be allocated at maximum size for every block, and when combined with many concurrent blocks in memory, exhausting JVM heap and crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: copy()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated `copy()` calls via `toBuilder().build()` on a `RecordFile` holding a large `byte[] bytes` array, causing shallow-copied byte array references to be retained across multiple `RecordFile` instances simultaneously in memory, multiplying heap usage and causing OOM in the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: clear()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file scenario where `clear()` only nulls `logsBloom` and replaces `sidecars` with `List.of()` but leaves `byte[] bytes` and `List<RecordItem> items` fully populated in memory, preventing GC from reclaiming large allocations and causing sustained heap pressure that degrades or crashes the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isEmpty()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the identity-based `isEmpty()` check (`this == EMPTY`) by triggering a code path that mutates the `RecordFile.EMPTY` singleton via Lombok-generated setters (since `@Data` generates public setters), causing downstream components that rely on `isEmpty()` to behave incorrectly and halt or loop indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: hapiVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that result in a `RecordFile` with `hapiVersionMajor`, `hapiVersionMinor`, and `hapiVersionPatch` all set to `Integer.MAX_VALUE`, causing `new Version(hapiVersionMajor, hapiVersionMinor, hapiVersionPatch)` to produce an overflow or unexpected comparison result that bypasses version-gated logic and triggers unbounded processing paths?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (gasUsed field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a large number of contract calls with maximum gas usage so that the `long gasUsed` accumulation in `RecordItemAggregator.accept()` overflows to a negative value, corrupting the `RecordFile.gasUsed` field and causing downstream consumers that rely on this value to enter error-retry loops that stall the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (count field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where `Long count` in `RecordFile` diverges from the actual `items.size()` (since `count` is set independently via `setCount()`), causing downstream processing loops that iterate `count` times to either under-process or over-iterate, leading to an infinite loop or index-out-of-bounds crash that halts the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (sidecarCount field)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a state where `int sidecarCount` is set to a large value while `Collection<SidecarFile> sidecars` remains empty (since they are set independently), causing downstream code that allocates data structures sized by `sidecarCount` to pre-allocate excessive memory and crash the importer with OOM?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (size field)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `RecordFile` where `Integer size` is set to a value far larger than the actual `bytes.length`, causing downstream code that uses `size` to allocate buffers or pre-size collections to exhaust memory, resulting in an OOM crash that halts the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (consensusEnd field as @Id)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a `consensusEnd` value of `Long.MAX_VALUE` or `Long.MIN_VALUE`, causing JPA/Hibernate to attempt to persist an out-of-range primary key that triggers a database error loop, stalling the importer's persistence pipeline indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (consensusStart field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce a `RecordFile` where `consensusStart` is greater than `consensusEnd`, violating the temporal ordering invariant and causing downstream date-range filters or metrics calculations (`Duration.between(consensusInstant, Instant.now())`) to produce negative durations that crash or corrupt metric recording?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (index field)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `RecordFile` with a `null` `Long index` to be processed, triggering a `NullPointerException` when `recordFile.getIndex()` is unboxed in downstream code (e.g., `recordFile.setIndex(recordFile.getIndex() + 1)`), crashing the importer thread and halting all record file processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (digestAlgorithm field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a record file with an unsupported or null `DigestAlgorithm` ordinal value, causing `@Enumerated` JPA mapping to throw an exception during persistence that triggers the `@Retryable` retry loop in `RecordFileParser.parse()`, exhausting retry budget and permanently stalling the importer on that file?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (version field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with an unsupported `version` integer (e.g., version 99) that passes initial validation but causes `CompositeRecordFileReader` to throw `InvalidStreamFileException`, which when combined with the `@Retryable` annotation on `RecordFileParser.parse()`, causes the importer to retry indefinitely and block processing of all subsequent record files?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (hash field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce a `RecordFile` with a `hash` string of extreme length (far exceeding the 96-character DB column limit), causing a database constraint violation during persistence that triggers the retry loop and permanently blocks the importer pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (previousHash field)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a `RecordFile` with a `previousHash` that does not match the `hash` of the preceding file, triggering a `HashMismatchException` that causes the importer to enter a retry loop via `@Retryable`, consuming CPU and blocking all subsequent file processing until the retry budget is exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (fileHash field)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a record file where `fileHash` is a null or empty string, causing downstream hash verification logic to throw a `NullPointerException` or `IllegalArgumentException` that is not caught, crashing the importer thread and halting all record file processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (metadataHash field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file where `metadataHash` (a `@Transient` field) is set to a value that causes signature verification to fail, triggering repeated re-reads and re-verifications of the same file in a loop that exhausts CPU and prevents the importer from advancing to the next file?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordFile.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: RecordFile (loadStart/loadEnd fields)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where `loadEnd` is less than `loadStart` in a `RecordFile`, causing the metric calculation `streamFile.getLoadEnd() - streamFile.getLoadStart()` in `AbstractStreamFileParser.parse()` to produce a negative duration that corrupts timing metrics and causes metric recording to throw an exception that halts the parser?
